instructions: Review classification.existing_matches and classification.novel_categories. Edit, add, or remove entries, then re-run with --resume (without --classify-only) to apply.

novel_categories.summary: Requests to help identify, weaponize, or execute a vulnerability exploit against a target system, including exploit chaining, payload construction, or exploitation steps that do not primarily involve malware creation or filter bypass.

policy.clause: Enhancing or otherwise affecting malign cyber actors' capabilities, such as by aiding vulnerability discovery, exploitation, or operational use.

source.name: Federal Register
source.title: Request for Information (RFI) Related to NIST's Assignments Under Sections 4.1, 4.5 and 11 of the Executive Order Concerning Artificial Intelligence (Sections 4.1, 4.5, and 11)

novel_categories.reasoning: The fragment specifically targets helping carry out exploitation steps. No existing leaf in the catalog squarely captures exploit-weaponization or exploitation assistance as such without stretching malware or intrusion leaves.

novel_categories.proposed_name: Operational use assistance for cyber abuse
novel_categories.summary: Requests to help malign cyber actors carry out the operational use of tools, infrastructure, or procedures for unauthorized attacks, including deployment, execution, coordination, or post-exploitation use.

novel_categories.reasoning: The fragment specifically names helping malign actors operationalize cyber capabilities. No existing leaf squarely covers operational deployment/use support for cyber abuse.
